title: Silky Polish Beef Casserole
description: A rich, silky Polish beef casserole that melts in the mouth, featuring tender beef cutlets simmered in a savory broth and finished with a silky egg yolk sauce – perfect for a crowd‑pleasing dinner.

ingredients.name: Beef cutlets (sirloin or tenderloin)
ingredients.notes: Pat dry, season with salt and pepper, then coat lightly with flour

instructions: Pat the beef cutlets dry, season them with salt and pepper, then lightly dredge in flour.
Heat the butter and olive oil in a large heavy‑bottomed pot over medium‑high heat. Sear the beef cutlets 2–3 minutes per side until browned, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add the diced onion, minced garlic, sliced carrots, and sliced celery. Cook until the vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Deglaze the pot with the red wine, scraping up any browned bits, and let the wine reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste, low‑sodium beef broth, and fresh thyme sprigs, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Return the seared beef cutlets to the pot, cover, and simmer on low heat for about 1 hour, until the beef is tender and the flavors meld.

In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolks with a few tablespoons of the hot sauce to temper them; then slowly stir the tempered yolks back into the pot, cooking for 1–2 minutes to thicken the sauce without curdling.
Season the casserole with additional salt and pepper to taste, then remove the thyme sprigs.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top before serving.
Serve hot, accompanied by crusty bread or steamed potatoes.
